sections.title: A: Use the word given in capitals to form a word
sections.instructions: Use the word given in capitals at the end of each line to form a word that fits in the gap.

items.sentence: There is still no adequate (1) ...... (EXPLAIN) why, several thousand years ago, cultures in both Egypt and South America saw the (2) ...... (INTRODUCE) of pyramids.

Was it coincidence? Many (3) ...... (SCIENCE) believe that it was, and state that there is no (4) ...... (POSSIBLE) at all that people from two distant continents traded information on (5) ...... (BUILD) techniques.

However, some (6) ...... (REVOLUTION) theories in recent years, based on the (7) ...... (DISCOVER) that the two cultures also share key myths, legends and beliefs, suggest that the (8) ...... (APPEAR) of pyramids on opposite sides of the world was no coincidence at all.
One (9) ...... (RESEARCH) working on this, Crystal Davis, said: 'The (10) ...... (IMPORTANT) of finding out the truth here cannot be exaggerated.'

sections.title: B: Match to make sentences
sections.instructions: Match each sentence beginning (1–8) with the correct ending (A–H).

items.sentence: The fax machine has broken **D** down, so I'm afraid you'll have to wait until tomorrow.
items.answer: D – down, so I'm afraid you'll have to wait until tomorrow.

items.sentence: A number of experiments have been carried **A** out by physicists which prove that time can speed up and slow down.
items.answer: A – out by physicists which prove that time can speed up and slow down.

items.sentence: Whoever came **F** up with that idea should have won the Nobel Prize.
items.answer: F – up with that idea should have won the Nobel Prize.

items.sentence: The electricity was cut **C** off for over three hours yesterday.
items.answer: C – off for over three hours yesterday.

items.sentence: I'd like to find **H** out much more about technology in the Bronze Age.
items.answer: H – out much more about technology in the Bronze Age.

items.sentence: This chemical gives **E** off a very nasty smell when it's heated.
items.answer: E – off a very nasty smell when it's heated.

items.sentence: Cancer treatments have come **B** on enormously in recent years.
items.answer: B – on enormously in recent years.

items.sentence: I wonder if you could put me **G** through to the Radiology Department, please.
items.answer: G – through to the Radiology Department, please.

sections.title: C: Complete the second sentence using the word given
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words in each gap.

items.sentence:
I can't wait until we do some experiments in the science lab. **forward**
I'm really ...... some experiments in the science lab.

Communication changed completely when the telephone was introduced. **with**
Communication changed completely ...... the telephone.

Dr Frankenstein was finally successful in bringing the monster to life. **managed**
Dr Frankenstein ...... the monster to life.

I can't understand how they built the Pyramids. **impossible**
I ...... how they built the Pyramids.

Fleming discovered penicillin as a result of a little luck. **resulted**
A little luck ...... penicillin.

Nurses ...... 'angels'.

Einstein eventually realised that he was wrong. **conclusion**
Einstein eventually ...... that he was wrong.

Our physics teacher tried to explain the Theory of Relativity. **attempt**
Our physics teacher ...... explaining the Theory of Relativity.

We'll save enough money and then we'll buy a computer. **soon**
We'll buy a computer ...... saved enough money.

'I'm going to set up the equipment in a minute.' '...... give you a hand?'
Do you know who ...... the fact that sound travels in waves?
I'm thinking of getting a fax .......
The ...... of the outbreak of the disease is still unknown.
Ten million text messages are sent on ...... every minute.
